maestro-setup.current_description: Scaffolds project context (product, tech stack, coding guidelines, product guidelines, workflow) and initializes track registry. Use for first-time project onboarding.

evals.query: I just cloned this repo and want to set up maestro for the first time -- need product definition, tech stack, and workflow config
initialize maestro in this project so we can start tracking work with tracks and implementation plans
set up the project context files (.maestro/context/) for this codebase -- it's a brownfield Django app
let's onboard this React/Node monorepo to the maestro workflow, fresh start
start fresh with maestro setup, reset everything and redo the interview
scaffold the .maestro directory with all the context files so other maestro commands work
this is a brand new project, I want to use maestro for plan-first development -- where do I start?
I need to configure maestro for my Swift iOS project before creating any tracks
can you set up maestro's product.md, tech-stack.md, and guidelines.md? first time doing this
run maestro setup with --reset, the tech stack changed and I want to regenerate everything
set up the local development environment for this project -- install dependencies, configure env vars
initialize a new git repository and add a .gitignore for a Python project
create a new Next.js project from scratch with TypeScript and Tailwind
configure ESLint, Prettier, and husky pre-commit hooks for the codebase
set up CI/CD pipelines with GitHub Actions for automated testing and deployment
install the project dependencies with bun install and get the dev server running
generate an AGENTS.md context file that describes this codebase for AI agents
scaffold a new React component using the project's component generator CLI
create a new feature track for implementing user authentication with OAuth
configure the Docker development environment with compose and volumes for hot reload

init.current_description: Generates AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md files using the WHAT/WHY/HOW framework. Explores the codebase and produces minimal (<100 line) context files with progressive disclosure.

evals.query: generate an AGENTS.md file for this repo so AI tools understand the codebase structure and build commands
create the CLAUDE.md context file for this project -- want it to cover tech stack, how to build, and key rules
I need AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md files that explain what this project is and how to work with it
rebuild the AGENTS.md from scratch, the codebase has changed significantly since it was generated
create context files so Claude automatically understands this repo when I open a new session
generate a CLAUDE.md with build commands, test commands, and coding conventions for this monorepo
regenerate context files with --reset, we migrated from npm to bun and the old CLAUDE.md is stale
make an AGENTS.md that covers the tech stack, how to run tests, and the progressive disclosure files in .maestro/context/
I want AGENTS.md generated using the WHAT/WHY/HOW framework for this Rust project
update the CLAUDE.md to reflect the current state of the codebase -- explore and regenerate it
write a README.md for this project explaining how to install and get started
create API documentation for the REST endpoints in the users module
generate a CONTRIBUTING.md guide for open source contributors
write a getting started guide for new developers joining the team
set up maestro context files for the project -- product.md, tech-stack.md, workflow.md
read the existing AGENTS.md file and tell me what it says about the build process
add a new section to the existing AGENTS.md about our database migration workflow
explain what AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md files are and how they help AI coding assistants
write inline code documentation and docstrings for the authentication module
create a project overview document for the product team to review

maestro-new-track.current_description: Create a new feature/bug track with spec and implementation plan. Interactive interview generates requirements spec, then phased TDD plan. Use when starting work on a new feature, bug fix, or chore.

evals.query: I want to add dark mode support to the app -- create a track for it with spec and plan
there's a login timeout bug on mobile, users get kicked out after 5 minutes even when active -- let's plan a fix
new feature: add real-time notifications with websockets so users see updates without refreshing
create a track to refactor the database connection pooling -- it's leaking connections under load
I need to build an admin dashboard with user management, analytics, and system health -- plan it out
start a new bug track: the file upload endpoint returns 413 for files under the size limit on Safari
new chore: upgrade all major dependencies (React 19, TypeScript 5.7, Next.js 15) with a migration plan
plan out the work for adding Stripe payment integration -- subscriptions, webhooks, and customer portal
let's create a spec and implementation plan for migrating our auth from session cookies to JWT
I want to spec out adding OAuth with Google and GitHub as login providers
I need to design a complex multi-tenant SaaS platform with per-tenant data isolation, custom domains, and RBAC
let's do deep discovery on building a HIPAA-compliant patient records system from the ground up
start implementing the dark_mode_20260301 track, the plan is ready
what's the status of all the current tracks? show me progress
review the auth_flow track implementation against the spec
help me brainstorm what features to build next for the MVP
write a PRD for the new reporting dashboard feature
set up maestro for this project -- first time using it
revert the implementation of the notifications track, it caused regressions
design an event-driven microservices architecture for our e-commerce platform with full ceremony spec

maestro-design.current_description: Deep discovery and specification for ambitious features. Full BMAD-inspired interview with classification, vision, journeys, domain analysis, and FR synthesis. Same output contract (spec.md + plan.md) as new-track but far richer. Use for multi-component systems, regulated domains, or unclear requirements.

evals.query: I need to design a complex multi-tenant SaaS platform with per-tenant data isolation, custom domains, and dynamic RBAC
let's do deep discovery on building a HIPAA-compliant patient portal -- we need to get the spec absolutely right
design an event-driven microservices architecture for our e-commerce platform with order processing, inventory, and payments
I want to build a real-time collaboration engine like Google Docs -- this needs thorough specification work with user journeys
deep dive into requirements for a PCI-DSS compliant financial transaction processing system with fraud detection
I need full ceremony specification for a regulatory compliance dashboard serving multiple jurisdictions
design a multi-region deployment strategy with automatic failover, data replication, and conflict resolution
this is a big project -- AI-powered content moderation pipeline with human review queues, appeals, and audit trail
we're building a workflow automation engine that lets non-technical users create complex business workflows -- need deep spec
design an ambitious SSO system with SAML, OIDC, SCIM provisioning, and MFA across 5 client applications
create a track for adding a dark mode toggle to the settings page -- pretty straightforward
I need to fix a bug where the login form doesn't validate email addresses properly
plan out upgrading React from 18 to 19 -- standard dependency migration chore
add a simple CRUD endpoint for managing user notification preferences
let's plan a small refactor of the utility functions into separate modules
create a track for cleaning up unused CSS classes and dead code
start implementing the auth_system_20260301 track, the plan is ready
what's the progress on the current tracks? show me a status report
review the implementation of the payment track against the spec
set up maestro for this project -- I haven't used it before

maestro-implement.current_description: Execute track tasks following TDD workflow. Single-agent by default, --team for parallel Agent Teams, Sub Agent Parallels. Use when ready to implement a planned track.

evals.query: ok the spec and plan look good, let's start implementing the auth_flow_20260228 track
I'm ready to build this out -- execute the dark_mode_20260301 track tasks
run the implementation for my current track in team mode with parallel kraken and spark workers
let's implement this feature, use --parallel with worktree isolation to speed things up
resume implementation from where we left off yesterday on the api_refactor track -- use --resume
I want to start coding the tasks from the plan we just created for the notifications feature
execute the payment_integration track, it has 3 phases and I want to go phase by phase
kick off implementation of the user_settings track using team mode with --team flag
let's build what's in the plan -- start the data_migration track in single-agent mode
the plan is approved, start implementing -- run through all the TDD cycles for the search track
let's plan out how to implement a user authentication system for my Django app
create a new feature track for refactoring the database connection pooling
show me the progress on all active tracks -- how far along are we?
I need to undo the last phase of implementation, there's a regression in user login
review the code changes from the auth track against the spec
write a unit test for the UserService.authenticate() method using vitest
how do I set up maestro for a new project? I've never used it before
note down that we decided to use PostgreSQL instead of MySQL for this project
design a complex RBAC system with dynamic permissions -- this needs deep discovery
generate an AGENTS.md for this codebase with build commands and conventions

maestro-review.current_description: Code review for a track against its spec and plan. Verifies implementation matches requirements, checks code quality and security.

evals.query: review the implementation of the auth_flow track against the spec -- make sure we covered all acceptance criteria
I finished implementing the dark_mode track, can you audit the code against the requirements?
do a code review on the current in-progress track -- check quality, security, and spec compliance
verify the payment_integration track is complete and all tests pass against the spec
review what we've built for the api_refactor track -- does it match the plan and cover edge cases?
run a maestro review on the notifications track before we consider it done
check the track implementation against the spec.md requirements -- use --current for the active one
validate that the user_settings track implementation covers all the acceptance criteria from spec.md
I want a review of the search_20260301 track -- intent match, code quality, test coverage, security
the track is done, let's review it and see if we need auto-fixes before marking complete
can you review this pull request? here's the link: github.com/org/repo/pull/123
look at my code in src/auth.ts and tell me if there are any bugs or performance issues
review the design document I wrote for the new notification feature
check if the API integration tests have adequate coverage of error scenarios
do a security audit of the entire codebase looking for OWASP top 10 vulnerabilities
peer review this function I wrote -- is the algorithm correct and efficient?
review the changelog entries before we cut the v2.0 release
check the test results from the last CI run and tell me what failed
create a new track to implement the review comments from the PM's feedback
look at the git diff and tell me if the changes look correct before I commit

maestro-status.current_description: Show track progress overview with phase/task completion stats, next actions, and blockers.

evals.query: how are we doing on the maestro tracks? give me an overview of all active work
what's the progress on all my tracks -- how many are done, in progress, pending?
show me where things stand with the current implementation -- which phase are we in?
are there any blockers on the current tracks? what's stalled?
give me a status report on all maestro tracks with completion percentages
what track should I work on next? show me the pending work
where did we leave off? what's the next task to implement?
show me a progress overview -- I need to know if the auth track is on schedule
what's left to do on the dark_mode track? how many tasks remain?
I'm starting a new session -- catch me up on track progress and what to do next
git status -- what files have changed?
what's the status of the GitHub Actions CI pipeline for the latest push?
show me the test results from the last test run
how many open issues do we have in the bug tracker?
what's the state of the staging deployment? is it healthy?
check if the build passed on the main branch
show me the PR review status on GitHub -- any pending approvals?
how much disk space is the project using?
what's the current Node.js version and are we on the latest LTS?
show me all the TODO comments in the codebase

maestro-note.current_description: Capture decisions, constraints, and context to persistent notepad. Priority notes are injected into every session and implementation run.

evals.query: remember that we decided to use Redis for session caching, not Memcached -- record this as a priority note
make a priority note: all API responses must include a request_id header for tracing
save this constraint to the notepad: the staging DB credentials rotate every 30 days
show me what's in the maestro notepad right now
clear the working memory section of the notepad, those items are resolved
note down that the legacy /v1/users endpoint is deprecated as of this sprint
prune the notepad -- some of the working memory items are stale and resolved
add to manual notes: always run migrations with --fake-initial on first deploy to a new env
record this decision to the notepad: we're going with JWT tokens over session cookies for auth
I want to capture a constraint -- max file upload size is 10MB per the product team's requirement
add a code comment to this function explaining the business logic behind the discount calculation
create a README section about how to set up the development environment
write API documentation for the new user management endpoints
I want to take notes while reading through this pull request
save this Claude conversation somewhere so I can reference it later
create a new maestro track for implementing the notification system
what does the product.md context file say about our target user personas?
update the CLAUDE.md with new build instructions after the bun migration
add a TODO comment in the code where we need to implement retry logic
log this error stack trace for debugging later: TypeError at line 42 in UserService.ts

maestro-revert.current_description: Git-aware revert of track, phase, or individual task. Safely undoes implementation with plan state rollback.

evals.query: undo the implementation of the auth_flow_20260228 track -- it broke login for existing users
roll back phase 2 of the current track, the database migration was wrong and corrupted indexes
revert the last task I implemented in the notifications track -- the test approach was completely flawed
I need to undo everything from the dark_mode track and re-implement from scratch
the payment_integration track caused regressions in checkout -- revert all of it
undo phase 3 of api_refactor track so we can rethink the caching strategy
roll back the user_settings track implementation, the spec was wrong and we need to redo it
revert the track so we can re-implement with the updated plan after the spec revision
I want to undo just task 1.3 from phase 1 of the search_20260301 track
take back all the maestro implementation changes from the notifications track commits
git revert the last commit I made -- it has a typo in the config
undo my last edit to server.ts -- I accidentally deleted a function
reset the staging database to the latest backup from last night
roll back the Docker deployment to the previous container image version
can you undo the changes I made to package.json? I don't want those dependencies
revert the pull request that was merged to main yesterday -- PR #247
I want to go back to the previous version of the OpenAPI schema
remove the feature flag for dark mode from the codebase entirely
delete the test files we created during the spike -- they're not needed anymore
restore the nginx.conf backup after the failed configuration change
